entry.title: S/4 Hana - Track on SO schedule line level both requested delivery date and committed delivery date
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am working for a Make to Order scenario project, my customer wants to trace in the sales order just for statistical purpose at schedule line level both  the initial requested delivery date of the customer and also the committed date (if the date requested by the customer is not feasible).

In the sales order there is not availability check and are not configured lead times; the Customer care should enter manually the requested delivery date for each sales order item (also eventually splitting quantity on different dates), then if after discussion with Planning the date is not feasible the Customer care will enter for each schedule line previously defined the committed date (the date on which the company commits to the customer to deliver the product).

There is any standard solution to satisfy this requirement? Otherwise I am thinking to add a new custom field "committed date" on schedule line tab of the sales order.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP Signavio February 2026 Release: SAP Signavio Process Insights and Intelligence
entry.content:
Understanding how processes truly run — and turning those insights into action — remains one of the biggest challenges for organizations today. Teams often spend more time preparing data, interpreting results, or working around technical limitations than actually improving performance.

With this release, we’re focusing on removing that friction. Across SAP Signavio Process Insights and Intelligence, the latest innovations help you analyze processes more flexibly, uncover root causes faster, and work only with the data that matters. The result is simpler decision-making and quicker, more confident action.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Model and analyze processes driven by business objects with Object-based data modeling

Real business processes rarely follow a single linear path. Orders, deliveries, invoices, and other business objects constantly interact, yet traditional case-based analysis often forces teams into rigid structures that capture only part of the story.

With object-based data modeling, you can now view these relationships in one unified model. By decoupling case logic from fixed data structures, you define your data model once and reuse it across multiple analyses, without duplicating or reshaping source data. This makes it far easier to explore how different parts of your business connect, shift perspectives depending on the question at hand, and adapt analyses as requirements evolve. Complex, end-to-end processes become more transparent and significantly easier to understand.

Object Based Data Modeling

Understand root causes and act faster with AI-assisted insights  (Beta)

Identifying performance issues is only the first step. Interpreting what’s driving them and and deciding what to fix first: this is where many teams lose time and momentum.

AI-assisted Root Cause Analysis helps bridge this gap by automatically translating analysis results into clear explanations and actionable recommendations. Instead of manually investigating dashboards or debating priorities, you receive contextual insights into why issues occur and which improvements are likely to deliver the greatest impact. This accelerates the journey from insight to action and helps ensure that improvement initiatives consistently focus on what matters most.

This capability is currently available in beta. If you’re interested in participating, we’d love to hear from you!

AI-assisted root cause analysis: Cause interpretation & next action recommendation

Interested to know more about Root Cause Analysis (RCA)? Check out our blog posts on RCA as well as RCA on event graphs!

SAP Signavio Process Insights

Use filtering to control what data is extracted for process analysis

Not every piece of ERP data is relevant for process performance analysis. Frankly, some information should never leave the source system at all. And, until now, teams often had limited control over what was extracted, leading to unnecessary data movement or additional governance work.

With enhanced filtering options, system owners can now exclude sensitive or irrelevant attributes directly within the extraction process. This allows organizations to better align with compliance requirements while also reducing data volume and focusing analyses on what truly adds value. The outcome is a more efficient, secure, and streamlined approach to process insights.

Explore process execution with interactive variants

First announced in the previous release (Nov. 2025), the Execution Variant (Previously Variant Explorer) is now available in SAP Signavio Process Insights, bringing a more interactive way to understand how processes actually are executed.

Embedded directly in the Process Flow page, it allows you to explore and compare process variants, filter for specific behaviors, and quickly see how different execution paths affect performance. With improved visuals, support for more variants, and the ability to intuitively include, exclude, and cluster flows, it’s now easier than ever to pinpoint inefficiencies and focus on the variants that matter most.

Together, these enhancements provide a clearer, execution-based view of your processes, this helping teams identify optimization opportunities faster and act with confidence.

Execution Variant

Click here to start a step-by-step enable now tutorial for this feature. 

Together, these updates are designed to make process analysis more adaptable, actionable, and aligned with real-world business needs, helping teams spend less time managing data and more time improving outcomes.

Want to know more about this release? Check out the highlight blog post and see what else we prepared for this release over the entire SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite! 

We are looking forward to seeing you on the SAP Signavio Community to share feedback, questions and discuss with your peers! This is your space! 

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community or alternatively watch our Release Webcast on Demand.

entry.title: Auto-move/reject candidates with multiple applications
entry.content:
Overview:

I have heard this requirement a few times now, where the client would like to prevent candidates from applying for multiple requisitions/jobs. SuccessFactors does not prevent / restrict a candidate from applying to multiple requisitions, however, in this blog I will show how you can automatically move (or reject) candidates in the additional requisitions to a new status (1st application stays intact).

Pre-requisites:

  • Access to create a new field in the candidate profile (provisioning or Manage templates in Admin centre)
  • Access to create a new field in the application template (provisioning or Manage templates in Admin centre)
  • Access to business rules for recruiting

The solution:

In the candidate profile, create a custom text field (example: First Requisition Applied) to store the requisition id. Use the same field id to create the same field in the application. As both these fields are used for internal/technical purposes only, you do not have to worry about providing overrides or permissions.

See screenshot below:

Fig 1: Candidate profile XML showing the new field

 

Fig 2: Application XML showing the new field

Once this is done, create a new business rule under ‘Job Application: Basic Business Rule Scenario’, with the below condition (and save it under 'On Save Rules' in Manage rules in Recruiting).

1. If the newly created field ‘First Requisition Applied’ is NULL, then copy the requisition id to this field.

This ensures that if the candidate is applying for the first time, the field will be blank and hence we copy the requisition id , from the requisition into the field in the application (which in-turn automatically copies it into the candidate profile field as the field id’s are the same). For our example, let us assume the first requisition id, which the candidate applied to is 3086.

2. Else if the newly created field (First Requisition Applied) is NOT equal to the requisition id, then move the candidate to a new status (including any rejection statuses).

This ensures from the 2nd job application onward the candidate is automatically moved to the required status (including any rejection statuses). Example: the 2nd job has a requisition id as 3049, and when the candidate applies for this, the system checks if 3086 (from 1.) is NOT equal to 3049 and automatically moves the candidate.

Fig 3: Sample business rule:

 

Fig 4: Candidate profile screenshot

Once this rule is activated, every time a candidate clicks on 'Submit' for their application, this rule is triggered.

This way, with no major customisations or middleware being involved, we can change a candidate status (or reject) them on any additional job applications while still retaining the first application as-is.

Additional scenarios:

Parent - Child Requisition :

If the first requisition that the candidate is applying to is a parent requisition and you would be later moving the candidate to a child requisition, please ensure

  1. there is an application field that is populated after the ‘default’ status in the parent req
  2. this field needs to have the attribute ‘forward-intact=true’
  3. add this field to the business rule (an AND condition to 2. Above), where you ensure this field is blank.
  4. this way when the candidate is moved to the child req, this field will still be populated, and the business rule above will not auto-move or auto-reject the candidate
  5. Please see screenshot below with an example, where I am ensuring the ‘Contract country’ is blank, along with the requisition id check (as detailed in point 2 above)

Fig 5: Screenshot of the business rule when a common field is used to check for the parent-child relation:

Reset checks:

An additional requirement I’ve had is to reset the check on additional applications every year ie., in a year the candidate can only apply to one application, they can apply to a new job the next year / recruitment cycle.

  1. To meet this requirement, you can use Integration Centre to wipe out the field value in the candidate profile (not application – but Candidate profile – the source!), every year/cycle.

Things to note:

  • The field in the candidate profile can be hidden from the candidate view (by not adding any permissions). However, this field will be visible to internal recruiters / hiring managers and cannot be hidden. However, it can be re-labelled in a way no one gets confused (example ‘First Requisition applied’ OR ‘Technical field’ etc.,) with the use of the field along with some internal comms / change management
  • Last note, as this is a custom solution, SAP support will not help with any issues / debugging.

Please refer to other KBA’s and the community for all other Q and A’s.

entry.title: Re: India Vendor Return: Delivery Challan for Intrastate...
entry.content:
Purpose of the Delivery Challan app
The app (transaction J1IGINTRASTO in classic terms) is used to generate a Delivery Challan document for goods movements within India, including vendor returns. Even though you already post the return movement (122 or 161) in MIGO, Indian GST regulations require a delivery challan to accompany the physical movement of goods back to the vendor. The challan serves as a legal document for transport and tax compliance, not just an internal SAP posting. It contains details like vendor, plant, material, and billing type, which are mandatory for GST audit trails.2

Why it is required after MIGO
MIGO (movement type 122 or 161) creates the material document for the return. The Delivery Challan app then references that material document to generate the statutory challan. Without this step, you would have the stock movement recorded but no GST-compliant transport document, which could cause issues during audits or inspections.

Your error (“Customer name not maintained / No Bill To party defined”)
This usually indicates missing vendor master data configuration or billing type assignment for delivery challan. The app requires vendor details to be set up correctly in configuration (e.g., Bill-to party, GST registration details). Check the SSCUI or IMG activity for Configuration for Delivery Challan for Vendor Returns and ensure vendor and billing type are mapped.

Role when e-Way Bill integration (scope item 78L) is not in scope
Even if you generate e-Way Bills manually on the government portal, the Delivery Challan app is still required.

Reason: The challan is the basis for e-Way Bill generation. You need the challan details (document number, date, material, quantity, vendor) to enter into the government portal.

So, the app remains part of the compliant process, even without automatic integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Phase model Work Orders
entry.content:
Hi,
The Phase Model process normally starts with a 'maintenance request' , so a 'notification' in SSAM.
If you want to turn this into a Work Order immediately, skipping some of the intermediate phases, look into the creation of a notification 'with emergency processing context'. When you set this in SSAM, a Work Order will be created locally and be 'ready for execution'.
S/4 help guide on processing context: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/c9e3772f8ded4db4b91...
SSAM Config: https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-configuration-guide/em...
Hope this helps.
D.

entry.title: Re: Spanish EC Sales and Purchase List reporting
entry.content:
Good day @Jessicaaguilar1 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is a public cloud standard situation. It happens because, in Run Statutory Reports, the Manage Tax Items step is a preparation and review workspace for reporting period and company. It basically shows a broader tax item before you then finally see the specifics as you mentioned in your case. See KBA 3356046 for more insights.

So, the report data model/analyser then applies that report logic for ES_ECSL_DCL and keeps only records that meet EC list criteria for example intra EU relevant transactions.

Note that the two views are not inconsistent, they just display different processing layers below:

  1. Manage Tay Items = operational pool for review and inclusion control
  2. Report model output = legally determined dataset after report rules have been implemented.

I suggest you check the following in your system:

  • Verify that non-EC documents are either excluded automatically by report logic later or you can manually exclude them if you need to.
  • Confirm you selected the exact report ID ES_ECSL_DCL in Run Statutory Reports and correct your reporting entity/date.
  • The analyser will serve you as the validation point for final data scope, not the raw item list.

In summary, Spanish EC Sales and Purchase List, Manage Tay Items will display more invoices than the final EC report. The system applies the final filtering at the output stage, which is why your analyser correctly shows only intra EU relevant invoices.

See following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/aef42ea288a54ff3aa0ae38d8a4da203.html?locale=en-US&q=Making+Additional+Settings+for+VAT+Return+spain 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/7b31b01e5e4046fa9152620fc6df4898.html?locale=en-US&q=Making+Additional+Settings+for+VAT+Return+spain

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Questions on flexible workflow in purchase order
entry.content:
Q1 – Senior buyer creating a PO shouldn’t route to buyer approval
Flexible Workflow uses responsibility rules defined in the Teams and Responsibilities app. To prevent escalation downward, you need to configure the agent determination so that the workflow checks the creator’s role/limit before assigning approval.

Practically, this means:

Define responsibility rules by amount thresholds and role. Ensure the workflow condition excludes the creator if they already hold the authority for that threshold.
Example: If a Senior Buyer creates a PO ≤ 5000, the workflow can be configured to skip Buyer approval and either auto-approve or move to the next higher step.

Q2 – Senior buyer has left, workflow shouldn’t get stuck
If a team member leaves, the workflow can stall unless you maintain the team.

Options:

  • Maintain Teams and Responsibilities: Remove the departed member and assign a replacement.
  • Escalation/Alternate Approvers: Configure escalation paths or alternate approvers in the workflow definition so that if no agent is found, it routes to the next higher role (e.g., Manager).
  • Dynamic Agent Determination: Use rules that check availability; if no Senior Buyer is assigned, the workflow skips to Manager.

Q3 – Employee master data without HR integration
Yes, you can use the Maintain Employees app to create employee records in S/4HANA without SuccessFactors integration. Later, if you enable SuccessFactors Employee Central integration, you need to:

  • Map existing employee IDs to SuccessFactors IDs.
  • Perform a reconciliation so that workflow responsibilities continue to work seamlessly.
  • Typically, you’d migrate or align the employee master data so that the SuccessFactors feed becomes the single source of truth, but existing workflow teams can be adjusted to reference the new IDs.

entry.title: Re: Manage Workflow for Purchase Requisitions app - skip...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your information.

I suppose you're looking for the following information, which is provided in the following SAP Help Portal document:

Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions

"You can choose to exclude restricted agents from being approvers of the purchase requisition. By default, the restricted agents are not excluded.

  • Exclude Requestors: By choosing this option, requestors or creators of purchase requisitions will not receive requisitions that are created by them for approval in their inbox.

  • Exclude Restricted Agents Determined by BAdI: By choosing this option, the agents set by the the BAdI Enhancement of Flexible Workflow Properties are excluded from being approvers of the workflow step.

After exclusion, if no recipients are determined, the workflow will move to error unless the step is marked optional.."

If this is not what you're looking for, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: How to unlink an identity from your SAP Universal ID
entry.content:
Learn how to unlink an identity (S-user ID/P-user) from your SAP Universal ID account. 

Steps on how to unlink an identity from an SAP Universal ID  
  
1. Sign into the SAP Account Manager.  

2. Navigate to the "Identities" section and locate the user to be unlinked.  

 

3. Select the "Unlink Identity" option from the three dots in the upper right corner of the chosen user.  

4. Click "Confirm" to complete the unlinking.  

 

 

You can also refer to KBA 3619621 How to unlink a user from an SAP Universal ID in SAP Account Manager? 

What to do if you encounter issues unlinking an identity 

If you cannot complete the above steps, please contact The Customer Interaction Center (CIC) by clicking here and provide the following details: 

  • Identity to be unlinked 
  • The reason for unlinking 
  • Proof of ownership (This request should be sent from the SAP Universal ID email address) 

You can find more details on SAP Universal ID deletion in this KBA 3539768 - How to delete an SAP Universal ID (UID)? or this; How to delete your SAP Accounts (P-User, S-User or Universal ID)  BLOG. 

Thanks for reading and I hope you found it useful!  

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community     

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: 10 reasons to trust the SAP Business Transformation Center with your data migration
entry.content:
Thank you for your questions @Atul_Joshi85 . Gives me the opportunity to add some thoughts and infos.

1. SDT versus Greenfield: Speaking personally, many Greenfield programs struggle: they take too long, cost a lot, and risk delivering processes that are already outdated by go-live. For many customers, it is more practical to get to the current platform (SAP S/4HANA) quickly with Selective Data Transition (SDT), then improve iteratively. The era of rolling out a template and leaving it unchanged for 10 years is over. My hope is that SAP BTC lowers the barrier for faster SDT migrations, so customers can move now and evolve continuously.

2. Clean Core and footprint reduction: The “cutoff date” depends on several factors (compliance, audit, finance, operations). SAP BTC helps by showing fact-based footprint reduction for different dates, so stakeholders can weigh benefits and trade-offs. In some cases, laws require certain data to stay in the S/4 system. It’s also important to stress that data is not “lost” if it is not migrated. With a proper decommissioning plan for ECC and, ideally, an ILM strategy, teams can still access older data when needed. Without filling S/4 with unnecessary history.

3. Security, residency, and the cloud: A careful approach to sensitive data is a core product standard for SAP BTC and SAP Cloud ALM in general. Features undergo explicit security reviews and audits. Explaining our architecture has already helped to convince “cloud-hesitant” stakeholders. 

4. Skills gap:  SAP BTC is not an expert-tool. Functional consultants and IT teams can use it using the guided, Fiori-based UX. You should still involve someone with Basis skills—especially for initial setup (onboarding ABAP systems ect.) and performance tuning during execution. But we also provide strong support for these steps. E.g. the Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS) for setup and a recently published Performance Guide for optimization.

5. Real-world content and edge cases: So far, the predefined BTC content has worked well in our projects and go-lives. Maybe als worth mentioning is that also BTC content can be updated daily, which lets us react quickly if issues arise. For truly unique situations, our Support has helped address customer-specific needs.

entry.title: Re: India Process Ewaybill for PO Return Delivery Questio...
entry.content: You Purchase Orders cannot be used for e‑Way Bill generation. GST requires a tax document (invoice, bill of supply, or delivery challan). If the vendor has not yet issued the invoice at the time of pickup, they can issue a delivery challan instead. This delivery challan serves as the reference document for you (buyer) to generate the e‑Way Bill. Later, when the invoice is issued, it becomes the commercial/tax document for accounting, but the challan covers the transport compliance at the time of pickup.
